---
title: "Expansion 3G plot"
author: Casey Breen
---

Summary: Create figure with 3g expansion in Nigeria 

```{r}
## Library packages 
library(here)
source(here("code", "helpers.R"))

## Birth panel 
mobile_coverage <- read_csv( here("data", "ng_adm0_coverage.csv"))

## birth panel 
birth_panel <- read_csv(here("data", "birth_panel.csv"))
```

## create plot 

```{r}
## Reshape data 
mobile_coverage_long <- mobile_coverage %>% 
  pivot_longer(-share_type) %>% 
  filter(name != "...1") %>% 
  mutate(year = sub('.*(\\d{4}).*', '\\1', name)) %>% 
  mutate(type = case_when(
    share_type == "pop_share" ~ "Population",
    share_type == "area_share" ~ "Land Area"
  )) %>% 
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year))
```


## Visualize 

```{r}
## Visualize plot 
expansion_3g_plot <- mobile_coverage_long %>% 
  filter(year %in% 2010:2018) %>% 
  mutate(label = if_else(year == max(year), as.character(type), NA_character_)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value * 100, color = type, fill = type, shape = type)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  geom_point(size = 3, stroke = 1, fill = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value * 100, 0)), vjust = -1.5, size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = label),
                  nudge_x = .85,
                  na.rm = TRUE,
                  segment.color = NA) + 
  cowplot::theme_cowplot() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2010:2018) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Population" = "darkblue", "Land Area" = "#CC6600")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Population" = "darkblue", "Land Area" = "#CC6600")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Population" = 21, "Land Area" = 23)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Coverage (%)", color = NULL, fill = NULL, shape = NULL) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  ylim(0, 66) + 
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black"),
        axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", colour = "black"))

# Save plot
ggsave(plot = expansion_3g_plot,
       filename = here("figures", "expansion_3g_plot.png"),
       height = 4, width = 7.3, bg = "white")
```



```{r}
# Create 5 bins: [0–20%), [20–40%), ..., [80–100%)x
birth_panel_filtered <- birth_panel %>%
  mutate(coverage_bin = cut(
    pop_share_covered_3g,
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
    include.lowest = TRUE,
    right = FALSE,
    labels = c("0–20%", "20–40%", "40–60%", "60–80%", "80–100%")))

# Summarize ownership and 3G by bin 
summary_by_bin <- birth_panel_filtered %>%
  filter(year == 2018, !is.na(pop_share_covered_3g)) %>%
  group_by(coverage_bin) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_phone_ownership = mean(v169a, na.rm = TRUE), 
    n = n(),
    mean_3G_coverage = mean(pop_share_covered_3g, na.rm = TRUE))


# Plot
mobile_phone_ownership_by_3g_coverage <- ggplot(summary_by_bin, aes(x = coverage_bin, y = mean_phone_ownership)) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightgrey", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(mean_phone_ownership, accuracy = 1)), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    x = "3G Coverage (Cluster Level)",
    y = "Mean Mobile Phone Ownership"
  ) +
  cowplot::theme_cowplot()


# Save plot
ggsave(plot = mobile_phone_ownership_by_3g_coverage,
       filename = here("figures", "mobile_phone_ownership_by_3g_coverage.png"),
       height = 4, width = 7, bg = "white")
```
